items.title: Bekerduel Bayern gaat ondanks grote staking toch door
items.summary: Bayern München heeft maandagmiddag gecommuniceerd dat de bekerwedstrijd van komende woensdag, tegen RB Leipzig, gewoon doorgaat. Duitse media waren onrustig, vanwege een grote staking in Duitsland stond het bekerduel volgens hen op losse schroeven.

items.title: Dumfries debuteert als schaatstoeschouwer en komt met WK-update
items.summary: Hoewel Denzel Dumfries een jaar bij SC Heerenveen heeft gevoetbald, was de rechtsback van Internazionale nog nooit bij een schaatswedstrijd geweest. Maandagavond kwam daar verandering in, op de Olympische Spelen in Milaan. Dumfries stond de NOS kort te woord.

items.title: Deense bond laat beslissing over WK-boycot aan de politiek
items.summary: Als Denemarken het WK haalt door in maart de play-offs te winnen, is het aan het nationale parlement om te beslissen of de ploeg ook echt naar het toernooi in Noord-Amerika afreist. Dat heeft de Deense voetbalbond DBU maandag besproken.

items.title: Monday Night Club
items.summary: Mark Chapman and guests discuss the weekend's football

items.title: Pakistan end T20 World Cup boycott and prepared to face India
items.summary: Pakistan have ended a proposed boycott for their T20 World Cup match against India and will play as scheduled this Sunday, live on Sky Sports.

items.title: Memphis biedt excuses aan voor door Cicinho bespotte penaltymisser
items.summary: Corinthians leed in de nacht van zondag op maandag een pijnlijke nederlaag in het staatskampioenschap van São Paulo, het zogeheten Campeonato Paulista. Palmeiras was met 1-0 te sterk , mede vanwege een door Memphis Depay gemiste penalty. Memphis bood zijn excuses inmiddels aan, maar werd ook bespot door ex-prof Cicinho.

items.summary: Columnist Nico Dijkshoorn koestert de tijd dat voetballers nog bleven staan bij een aanraking.

items.title: 'Furious' derby is start to '13 cup finals' for leaders Hearts
items.summary: Tuesday's Edinburgh derby could go a long way to deciding whether Heart of Midlothian will end a 66-year wait to win another Scottish title.

items.title: Sources: Cristiano Ronaldo ends Al Nassr dispute
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has decided to end his boycott of Al Nassr games, sources have told ESPN Brasil.

items.title: Laporta resigns as Barça president, will re-stand
items.summary: Joan Laporta has resigned as Barcelona president ahead of re-standing for the role when club members go to the polls on March 15.

items.title: Cazorla: 'LaLiga light years away from the PL'
items.summary: Santi Cazorla has taken a swipe at LaLiga after his team's league game at Rayo Vallecano on Saturday afternoon was postponed just hours before kickoff.

items.title: Foster: Wrexham's journey 'ridiculous'
items.summary: Ben Foster has hailed Wrexham's "ridiculous" journey as Hollywood owners Rob Mac and Ryan Reynolds celebrate their fifth anniversary in charge of the Welsh club.

Watford appoint Edward Still as head coach
items.summary: Watford appoint Edward Still as head coach following the resignation of Javi Gracia after only three months in charge.

items.summary: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on golf superstar Ian Poulter plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this week's midweek Premier League fixtures.

items.title: You may not like the Liverpool red card, but it was the right call | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Referee Craig Pawson sent off Dominik Szoboszlai by the letter of the law; the only way it should be done Sign up for Soccer with Jonathan Wilson here Refereeing is the most thankless of jobs. There are times when you can get a decision absolutely right and still you get criticised on all sides. In the final seconds at Anfield on Sunday, with the Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson caught upfield, Rayan Cherki rolled the ball towards the Liverpool goal. Erling Haaland gave chase and would have gotten there to nudge the ball definitively over the line but he was pulled back by Dominik Szoboszlai, who would then have caught up with the ball to clear had he not been pulled back by Haaland. The ball crossed the line but the referee Craig Pawson, after a VAR review, gave not a goal but a free-kick for the first offence, sending Szoboszlai off for the denial of an obvious goal-scoring opportunity. Continue reading...

items.title: Uproar in the Maldives as football relegation battle decided by last-day no-show
items.summary: Green Streets failed to play last game of season Team stayed up on goal difference, rivals relegated There is uproar in the Dhiraagu Dhivehi Premier League, the leading football competition of the Maldives, after it was alleged one of the tropical archipelago’s leading clubs sought to escape relegation by failing to play a match. The Premier League side Green Streets beat the drop last Thursday after not fulfilling their final league fixture of the season against New Radiant. Continue reading...
